mechanism_narrative: A lower federal funds rate reduces discount rates and eases financial conditions, so falling FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The effect is strongest when the rate is already near the effective lower bound and policy stays unchanged rather than tightening.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d SPX forward returns are not higher after months with declining or flat FEDFUNDS than after rising FEDFUNDS, or if the sign reverses in more than 60% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals weakening aggregate demand and earnings pressure, which should depress equity prices over the next quarter. When UNRATE is accelerating, the market should price a deeper slowdown and generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes produce positive 60d SPX returns in more than 55% of cases, or if the average forward return is not below the unconditional mean by at least 1 standard deviation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly rise in retail sales captures an inflection in consumer spending, supporting revenue growth and near-term equity gains. The signal should work when RSAFS accelerates faster than its recent trend, indicating improving demand momentum rather than inflation alone.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS monthly change exceeds its prior 6-month average change by 1 standard deviation
failure_condition: Reject if high-RSAFS-momentum months do not lead to positive 5d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal’s hit rate falls below 52% over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production rises while inflation remains contained, it signals real output growth without an immediate policy or margin squeeze, which should support equities over the next month. The mechanism fails if output gains are accompanied by a sharp CPI acceleration that forces tighter financial conditions.
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO changes paired with low CPI inflation do not outperform all other months on 20d SPX returns, or if the interaction term is not positive and significant in rolling out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts are a leading indicator of construction activity, household formation, and credit sensitivity; sustained increases should lift cyclical equity expectations over the next quarter. The signal is strongest when starts are rising from depressed levels, which indicates recovery rather than overheating.
failure_condition: Reject if three-month rising HOUST sequences do not produce above-baseline 60d SPX returns, or if the average post-trigger return is non-positive across two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation surge with policy rates still near zero signals a real-rate squeeze and rising fear of future tightening, which should hurt equities over the next month. The negative effect is strongest when CPI accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains pinned, because the market loses confidence in policy support.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized inflation rises above 3% while FEDFUNDS stays below 0.5%
failure_condition: Reject if high-inflation/low-rate triggers do not correspond to lower 20d SPX returns than non-triggered periods, or if the interaction effect becomes positive in rolling out-of-sample estimation.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth and falling unemployment together confirm that labor demand is broadening rather than offset by labor-force shrinkage, which should support equities over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not independent when only one labor series improves; both must move in the same direction to validate a true cyclical upswing.
failure_condition: Reject if simultaneous PAYEMS gains and UNRATE declines fail to beat the unconditional 20d SPX return distribution, or if either series alone explains the effect as well as the interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth after a weak prior quarter indicates upside growth surprise, which should lift equities over the following quarter through earnings revisions and risk appetite. The effect requires an actual acceleration versus the previous GDP reading, not simply a high level of output.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 grows faster than the prior quarter by at least 0.5pp annualized
failure_condition: Reject if GDP acceleration episodes do not outperform other quarters on 60d SPX returns, or if the sign is negative in more than half of post-revision backtests using only data available at the decision date.
